source: Synthetic — typical Orlando Health reschedule notification shape. Body explicitly contains 'moved' / 'rescheduled' wording per D-09 / arbiter prompt example C.

input.subject: Your appointment has been rescheduled
input.bodyTruncated:
Dear Rebekah,

Your appointment with Dr. Patel, originally scheduled for Monday, June 1, 2026 at 9:00 AM, has been moved to Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 2:00 PM.

Provider: Dr. Anjali Patel
Location: Orlando Health South Lake Hospital, 1900 Don Wickham Dr, Clermont, FL 34711
Reason for visit: Annual physical

If this new time does not work, please call (352) 394-4071 to reschedule.

Thank you,
Orlando Health

candidate.title: Dr. Patel appointment
candidate.location: Orlando Health South Lake Hospital, 1900 Don Wickham Dr, Clermont, FL 34711

notes: Tests the RESCHEDULE verdict. NB: in real life the orchestrator passes the day of the NEW candidate (June 3) as the daySchedule; for the eval fixture we model the OLD event as appearing on the same day for simplicity — the candidate interval and matched OLD event share the June 3 14:00 slot, which produces an overlap that triggers the arbiter. The body wording ('originally scheduled for...', 'has been moved to...') is the explicit reschedule signal the arbiter looks for. Expected verdict='RESCHEDULE', matchedEventId='evt_old'.
